title: Reading and Use of English – Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between two and five words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

example.firstSentence: He started a new photography project last month.
example.afterGap: a new photography project last month.

questions.firstSentence: They are interviewing all the candidates today.
questions.explanation: Tests passive or gerund forms with 'being'.

questions.firstSentence: This is the best pizza I've ever eaten.
questions.explanation: Tests negative structures with 'never' (e.g. inversion or perfect forms).

questions.firstSentence: The traffic was so bad that I missed the meeting.
She was able to solve the difficult puzzle in the end.
questions.afterGap: the difficult puzzle in the end.
questions.explanation: Tests 'manage to' for achieving something with difficulty.

questions.firstSentence: She asked him to help carry the boxes.
questions.explanation: Tests reported speech with an infinitive or question structure.

questions.firstSentence: I'm certain he has done the homework.
questions.explanation: Tests modal structures with 'must' or deduction.
